1000627768.english: Hahaha. Commander, you want the information, don't you?

1000627769.english: Then... there are a few conditions, aren't there? Hehe, hehehehe...

1000627770.english: - After obtaining video information that Aurora entered Hachiko's room...
1000627770.korean: - 아우로라가 하치코의 방으로 들어갔다는 영상 정보를 입수하고...

1000627772.english: Can I really have more snacks?

1000627773.english: Wow! Master, you're the best... Ugh, but... if I do that, Pero will scold me...
1000627773.korean: 와아! 주인님 최고... 아으, 그런데... 그러면 페로한테 혼나요...

1000627774.english: It's heartbreaking, but sending Hachiko far away from the room...

1000627777.english: - Finally, I was able to corner... no, meet Aurora.
1000627777.korean: - 마침내 아우로라를 궁지에 몰아넣... 아니, 만날 수 있었다.

1000627779.english: - Aurora's fingertips are trembling as she tries to smile.
1000627779.korean: - 애써 웃는 아우로라의 손끝이 떨리고 있다.

1000627780.english: - It's scary to face a painful past. But...
1000627780.korean: - 아픈 과거와 마주하는 것은 두렵다. 하지만...

1000627781.english: - If you stay in that state forever, you won't be able to move forward.
1000627781.korean: - 언제까지나 그 상태여서야, 앞으로 나아가지 못한다.

1000627783.korean: - 내가 다가서자 아우로라는 벌벌 몸을 떨었다.

1000627784.english: - I forced myself to calm down and started talking.

1000627786.english: Shishishi time?! Shi, you mean time?!

1000627788.english: - I steeled my resolve as I watched Aurora lower her head and answer in a low voice.
1000627788.korean: - 기어들어가는 목소리로 대답하며 고개를 푹 숙이는 아우로라를 보며 마음을 굳혔다.

1000627791.english: - If you want to have a proper conversation... there's no other place than the Choco Queen's castle.
1000627791.korean: - 제대로 이야기를 나누려면... 역시 초코 여왕의 성밖에 없다.

1000627792.english: - Aurora, who had been pondering my words for a moment, nodded slightly.
1000627792.korean: - 내 말에 잠시 고민하던 아우로라는 작게 고개를 끄덕였다.

1000627793.english: Yeah. I've been practicing a lot...

1000627794.english: Hehehe, this might be more helpful than last time.
1000627794.korean: 헤헷, 저번보다 도움이 될 수 있을 거야.

1000627795.english: - Aurora showed a bright smile for the first time in a long time.
1000627795.korean: - 아우로라는 정말로 오랜만에 환한 웃음을 보여주었다.

1000627798.english: - At first glance, Aurora's field manipulation and power were incomparably higher than before.
1000627798.korean: - 한 눈에 보기에도 아우로라의 역장 조작과 파워는 이전과는 비교도 되지 않을 정도로 상승해 있었다.

1000627802.english: - Aurora carefully placed the wrapped scone in my hand and bowed her head.
1000627802.korean: - 아우로라는 포장된 스콘을 조심스레 내 손에 올려놓고는 고개를 푹 숙였다.

1000627804.english: - As Aurora got closer to the castle, her words became increasingly quiet.
1000627804.korean: - 아우로라는 성에 가까워질수록 급격히 말수가 줄어들었다.

1000627805.english: - I arrived at the castle safely, passed through the courtyard, and climbed the stairs.
1000627805.korean: - 무사히 성에 도착해 안뜰을 지나 계단을 올랐다.

1000627806.english: - Emily, who was caught up in her sisters' prank, opened the door that showed traces of having been locked with a chain and entered the Choco Queen's room.
1000627806.korean: - 언니들의 장난에 휘말린 에밀리가 쇠사슬로 잠궜던 흔적이 있는 문을 열고 초코 여왕의 방으로 들어왔다.

1000627807.english: - It looks like they cleaned the place when it was used as a chocolate factory, as even the bedding was neatly organized.
1000627807.korean: - 성을 초코 공장으로 사용할 때 청소라도 한 모양인지, 침구류까지 깔끔하게 정리되어 있다.

1000627809.english: - I went to the Choco Queen panel I had seen before and turned it on.
1000627809.korean: - 예전에 보았던 초코 여왕의 패널로 가서 전원을 켰다.

1000627810.english: Commander... I'm not mentally ready yet...
1000627810.korean: 사, 사령관... 나 아직, 마음의 준비가 안됐, 는데...

1000627811.english: Ugh, ugh... I'm, I'm sorry. I can't do it yet...
1000627811.korean: 으, 으으... 미, 미안해. 난 아직 안되겠어...

1000627814.english: - Aurora took a deep breath as if she had made up her mind...

1000627815.english: - I sat on the bed with my eyes tightly closed and my arms spread out.
1000627815.korean: - 눈을 꼭 감고 침대에 앉은 채로 양 팔을 벌렸다.

1000627816.english: Yeah, I don't know much about this stuff...
1000627816.korean: 있지, 나 이런 건 잘 모르니까...

1000627818.english: - In the spacious room, only Aurora and I exchanged blank gazes.
1000627818.korean: - 넓은 방 안에서 나와 아우로라의 멍한 시선만이 교차했다.

1000627821.english: - It didn't take long for them to realize that there had been a serious misunderstanding between them.
1000627821.korean: - 서로간에 중대한 오해가 있었다는 것을 깨닫는 데에는 얼마 걸리지 않았다.

1000627823.english: That much... because the commander thought about it seriously.
1000627823.korean: 그만큼... 사령관이 진지하게 생각해 준 거니까.

1000627825.english: Last time I went to the commander's office...

1000627830.english: Hey, you! If you keep doing that, I'll stop you!
1000627830.korean: 다, 당신! 자꾸 그러면 못 하게 할 거야!

1000627832.english: Then I'll be the commander all by myself~

1000627841.english: I want to be with you...

1000627844.english: Haaah... It feels like I'm being held by my master...

1000627845.english: Master... Rize... I can't take it anymore...

1000627847.english: Even letting me into my master's bedroom was a huge concession.
1000627847.korean: 주인님의 침실에 들어오게 해준 것도 많이 양보한 거야.

1000627848.english: If you keep needlessly getting your scent on me any more than that, I'll drag you out immediately.
1000627848.korean: 그이상 쓸데없이 네 냄새를 묻혀대면 바로 끌고 나가겠어.

1000627851.english: It's completely lost its mind again.

1000627854.english: Because of things like that... I, I naturally...
1000627854.korean: 그런 일들이 있어서... 나, 나는 당연히...

1000627855.english: Leaving the previous one aside, Lilith and Rize... I knew they’d been hanging out together lately.
1000627855.korean: - 앞에 거야 그렇다치고, 리리스랑 리제 이녀석들... 요새 어울려다닌다 싶더라니.

1000627856.english: There was a misunderstanding, but it's true that Aurora had that record, isn't it?
1000627856.korean: - 오해가 있기는 했지만 아우로라가 그 기록을 갖고 있었던 것은 사실이잖아?

1000627857.english: I couldn't finish my sentence because of the sudden sweet scent.
1000627857.korean: - 훅 끼치는 달콤한 향기에 말을 끝맺지 못했다.

1000627858.english: Aurora, held in my arms, hugged my waist tightly with both arms.
1000627858.korean: - 품에 안긴 아우로라가 내 허리를 양팔로 꼬옥 끌어안았다.

1000627859.english: Thank you for worrying about me.

1000627861.english: Aurora nodded without saying a word.

1000627862.english: I was a bit shocked, but... it's okay.

1000627863.english: Because it wasn't just those cruel facts that were written.
1000627863.korean: 그런 잔혹한 사실만 적혀 있던 건 아니니까.

1000627864.english: I believe that the person known as the "Chocolate Queen" genuinely wanted to make others happy with sweets.
1000627864.korean: 그 초코 여왕이라는 분, 과자로 다른 사람들을 행복하게 만들어 주고 싶었던 마음만은 진짜라고 생각해.

1000627865.english: Aurora took out a individually wrapped chocolate from her bosom.
1000627865.korean: - 아우로라는 품 속에서 낱개로 포장된 초콜릿을 하나 꺼냈다.

1000627866.english: made it according to the remaining recipe. Commander... you'll be able to feel it if you taste it.
1000627866.korean: 남아있던 레시피대로 만들어봤어. 사령관도... 먹어보면 느낄 수 있을 거야.

1000627867.english: Commander. Will you... let me have a sweet dream...?
1000627867.korean: 사령관. 내가... 달콤한 꿈을 꿀 수 있게 해줄래...?

1000627868.english: Aurora, having removed the chocolate wrapper, looked at me and took a small bite.
1000627868.korean: - 초콜릿의 포장지를 벗긴 아우로라가, 나를 보며 그것을 살짝 입에 물었다.

1000627871.english: How's the kitchen work? Doesn't Sowan bother you?

1000627876.english: Oh, isn't it still a long way to go until cleaning time?

1000627877.english: Um... yeah. You don't really need to clean it that many times a day, right?
1000627877.korean: 음... 그래. 그렇게 하루에도 몇 번씩 청소할 필요는 없지 않아?

1000627879.english: It must be difficult in many ways, right?

1000627881.english: If you don't want to get in trouble again, first of all...

1000627883.english: Then I guess I'll have to wait inside.

1000627888.english: ...Let's sit quietly on the chair and wait.

1000627890.english: Hmm. At first glance, there doesn't seem to be a problem.

1000627892.english: It's not even a blanket, so what the heck...!

1000627894.english: Oh, that was a close call.

1000627895.english: Still, it's good that we know the cause.

1000627899.english: I don't see Aurora. Is she in the kitchen?

1000627901.english: Aurora seems a bit... strange these days, doesn't she?

1000627908.english: It's okay, since you helped me. I'll explain it to you.
1000627908.korean: 나를 도와준 거니까 괜찮아. 잘 말해줄게.

1000627912.english: Yes, it absolutely has to be that way.

1000627913.english: I'll be taking some extra members with me on the way... Will you help me with the battle?
1000627913.korean: 가는 길엔 따로 대원들을 데려갈건데... 전투, 도와줄래?

1000627914.english: That's perfect. Just sit over there for a moment.
1000627914.korean: 마침 잘 됐네. 저기 잠깐만 앉아 있어.

1000627920.english: But why did you think that?

1000627925.english: No, I didn't say anything like that...?

1000627926.english: That bed is dangerous. I need to investigate with the commander's authority.
1000627926.korean: 저 침대는 위험해. 사령관 권한으로 조사해봐야겠어.

1000627928.english: Hehehe, it really is true that they say things start with friends. Yeah. I like the Commander a lot too, so now we're not friends, but lovers- Huh? What, marriage?! Oh, it's not that I don't like it, but, wouldn't it be better to take things a little further? I'm mentally prepared, but that's a different story, and that's... Okay, please take care of me...
1000627928.korean: 에헤헷, 친구부터 시작한다는 말이 진짜 맞는 거였구나. 응. 나도 사령관 많이 좋아하니까, 이제 친구가 아니라 애인으로- 어…? 겨, 결혼인 거야?! 아, 아니 싫은 건 아닌데, 그, 그런 건 좀 더 단계를 밟아가는 게 좋지 않을까요 마음의 준비는 되어있지만 그거랑은 좀 다른 이야기이기도 하고 그게… 자, 잘 부탁드립니다…

1000627929.english: Nyahahaha. You seem confident, don't you? But this time... it'll be different than before. I'm going to check if you're truly... worthy of handling Poi. So... let's do it quickly♡&n
1000627929.korean: 냐하하핫. 자신 있으신가 보네요? 그런데 이번은… 지금까지랑은 다를 거예요. 주인님이 진짜로… 포이를 감당하실만한 자격이 있는지… 확인해볼 테니까요. 그러니까… 빨리하자♡

1000627930.english: - After running away from the bar, we've been up all night for several days.
1000627930.korean: - 술집에서 도망친 뒤, 우리는 며칠째 밤을 새고 있다.

1000627931.english: - I thought I'd find out who the murderer was thanks to Tomo's shocking remarks, but...
1000627931.korean: - 토모의 충격적인 발언으로 살인사건의 범인까지 알아내나 했지만...

1000627933.english: Tomo, I think I know who the culprit is!
1000627933.korean: 토모, 범인이 누군지 알 것 같아!

1000627937.english: I knew it. Just pick whatever you want to eat.
1000627937.korean: 그럴 줄 알았지. 먹고 싶은 메뉴나 골라.

1000627938.english: I want a hamburger! I want 100 hamburgers!

1000627939.english: Please take a look. I don't have the money to order 100 of them.
1000627939.korean: 좀 봐 달라고. 100개나 시킬 돈은 없어.

1000627940.english: Instead, I'll order one set and one single item.
1000627940.korean: 대신 특별히 세트 하나 단품 하나 시켜줄게.

1000627945.english: - After a small celebration of hamburgers and beer, we organized the information we had gathered.
1000627945.korean: - 햄버거에 맥주로 조촐한 자축파티를 한 뒤 우리는 그동안 얻은 자료를 정리했다.

1000627946.english: - I don't think they've completely caught up, but since they caused such a ruckus in the bar, it wouldn't be surprising if they got caught at any time.
1000627946.korean: - 완전히 꼬리가 잡히지는 않았다고 생각하지만, 술집에서 그런 난리를 쳤으니 언제 들켜도 이상하지 않다.

1000627949.english: - I rubbed my sleepy eyes as I watched Sherlock cover Tomo, who was curled up sleeping on the sofa, with a blanket.
1000627949.korean: - 소파에서 웅크려 자는 토모에게 담요를 덮어주는 셜록을 보며 졸린 눈을 비볐다.

1000627950.english: - Even I was a little tired from the series of events and all-nighters that followed after I logged into the game, but Sherlock showed no sign of it.
1000627950.korean: - 게임에 접속하고 나서 계속해서 이어진 사건과 밤샘에 나조차도 조금 피곤할 정도였지만, 셜록에게선 조금도 그런 기색이 보이지 않았다.

1000627951.english: Yeah, I was going to talk about that anyway.

1000627952.english: - Sherlock lined up the few photos I had taken before the commotion at the Kohei Church.
1000627952.korean: - 셜록은 내가 코헤이 교단에서 소동이 벌어지기 전에 찍은 몇 장 안되는 사진을 주르륵 늘어놓았다.

1000627953.english: Here, something very important is printed.

1000627954.english: - When Sherlock zoomed in on the photo, he saw some people between the palms of a woman trying to block the lens.
1000627954.korean: - 셜록이 사진을 확대하자 렌즈를 막으려는 여성의 손바닥 사이에 어떤 사람들이 찍혀 있었다.

1000627955.english: This man here is an executive of Densetsu. And next to him are the Prime Minister's wife and daughter.
1000627955.korean: 여기 이 남자, 덴세츠의 임원이다. 그리고 그 옆에 있는 건 총리대신의 아내와 딸이지.

1000627956.english: The executives of Densetsu and the Prime Minister's family were present.
1000627956.korean: 무려 덴세츠의 임원과 총리대신의 가족이 함께 있었던거다.

1000627957.english: I'd always suspected it wasn't just a simple cult, but this confirms it. The Kohei cult also engages in secret meetings between powerful figures.
1000627957.korean: 전부터 단순한 사이비 교단은 아닌 것 같다고 생각하고 있긴 했지만, 이걸로 확실해졌다. 코헤이 교단에선 권력자들의 밀회 또한 이루어진다.

1000627958.english: Even though the resolution is a bit low, this photo alone is a huge scandal. As expected, Watson, you're definitely lucky.
1000627958.korean: 해상도가 좀 떨어지긴 해도 이 사진 한 장만으로도 대단한 스캔들이다. 역시 왓슨 자네에겐 행운의 여신이 함께하는 게 틀림없어.

1000627959.english: - Well, it wouldn't be wrong to say that I'm the luckiest person in the world...
1000627959.korean: - 세상에서 가장 운이 좋은 인간이라고 해도 틀린 말은 아니니까 뭐...

1000627963.english: Just do one thing, sleep or ask...

1000627964.english: Since I already sent it to the editor, as soon as it's confirmed- Ah, I see.
1000627964.korean: 일단 편집장한테 보내놨으니까, 컨펌만 나면 바로- 아, 그렇군.

1000627965.english: Watson, could you take this and print out some pictures for me?
1000627965.korean: 왓슨, 이거 가져가서 사진들을 좀 인쇄해주겠나?

1000627966.english: - When I asked him while accepting the USB, Sherlock put his hand to his forehead as if he was in shock.
1000627966.korean: - USB를 받아들며 묻자 셜록은 진절머리가 난다는듯 이마를 짚었다.

1000627967.english: Our superiors are so conservative that we have to write reports by hand and upload them separately, but the printer broke down.
1000627967.korean: 우리 윗분들이 꼰... 보수적이라서 수기로 보고서를 작성해서 따로 올려야하는데, 프린터가 고장나버렸어.

1000627968.english: You should be fine if you go to the convenience store in front of the station. Tomo-
1000627968.korean: 역 앞 편의점에 가면 될거다. 길은 토모가-

1000627972.english: I stepped out onto the city streets that were just waking up.
1000627972.korean: - 이제 막 깨어나는 도심의 거리로 나왔다.

1000627973.english: Tomo poked me in the side while I was distracted looking around.
1000627973.korean: - 주위를 둘러보느라 정신 없는 내 옆구리를 토모가 쿡쿡 찔렀다.

1000627974.english: Watson, you look like someone visiting the city for the first time!
1000627974.korean: 왓슨, 도시 처음 와보는 사람 같아!

1000627976.english: Was it because he thought it was just a game anyway? Tomo, who had been clutching his stomach laughing at the remark I had unwittingly let out, raised his head and looked up at me.
1000627976.korean: - 어차피 게임일 뿐이라는 생각이 들어서였을까. 나도 모르게 흘린 말에 배를 잡고 웃던 토모가 고개를 들어 나를 올려다보았다.

1000627977.english: Hey, Watson. Are you having fun with Tomo and Sherlock?
1000627977.korean: 있지, 왓슨. 왓슨은 토모랑 셜록이랑 있어서 즐거워?

1000627978.english: Yeah yeah! Tomo is having so much fun too!

1000627979.english: Hehehe, then Tomo will have fun for Watson's sake too!
1000627979.korean: 에헤헷, 그러면 토모가 왓슨 몫까지 즐거워할래!

1000627980.english: We arrived at the convenience store while chatting for a while.

1000627981.english: Tomo entered the shop, which looked like a scaled-down version of the Saman Industrial Materials Sales Office in Orca, and familiarly operated the printer in the corner.
1000627981.korean: - 오르카에 있는 삼안 산업 물자 영업소를 줄여놓은 듯한 가게 안으로 들어선 토모는 익숙하게 구석에 있는 프린터를 조작했다.

1000627982.english: While aimlessly looking around the store, I spotted a familiar face on the TV.
1000627982.korean: - 하릴없이 매장내를 둘러보던 중, TV에서 익숙한 얼굴을 발견했다.

1000627983.english: What kind of story will unfold next? Everyone, please give strength to Momo and Baekto!
1000627983.korean: 다음엔 어떤 이야기가 펼쳐질까요? 다들, 모모와 백토에게 힘을 나눠줘요!

1000627984.english: And the next broadcast is... Ta-da! It's a rerun of Red Arena Part 7. See you next time~
1000627984.korean: 그리고 다음 방송은... 짠! 붉은 아레나 7편의 재방송이에요. 그럼 다음에 만나요~

1000627985.english: It comes to mind again, but Momo... was a magical girl...

1000627986.english: Ah, I forgot to watch Momo...

1000627987.english: I'm going to ask Sherlock to buy me the replay!

1000627988.english: Before I could even say anything, Tomo wandered around the store humming a tune, seemingly having lost interest in no time.
1000627988.korean: - 내가 뭐라고 말하기도 전에 토모는 금세 흥미를 잃어버린듯 콧노래를 부르며 매장 곳곳을 돌아다녔다.

1000627989.english: The numerous food items on display catch my eye. Oh, this looks quite tasty. I should ask the restaurant team—
1000627989.korean: - 진열되어있는 수많은 먹거리들이 눈에 들어온다. 오, 이건 꽤 맛있을 것 같은데. 식당팀에 물어봐야겠-

1000627990.english: spotted a familiar face again in a magazine tucked away next to the instant foods.
1000627990.korean: - 즉석식품들 옆에 꽂혀있는 잡지에서 또다시 익숙한 얼굴을 발견했다.

1000627991.english: - He took out the magazine featuring Laviata on the cover and casually flipped through the pages.
1000627991.korean: - 라비아타가 표지를 장식하고 있는 잡지를 꺼내어 대충 페이지를 넘겼다.

1000627992.english: Next to Eva's photo is the face of a man I've never seen before. Who is this?
1000627992.korean: - 에바의 사진 옆엔 처음 보는 남자의 얼굴. 이건 누구지?

1000627995.english: Yeah. He's a male bioroid made by Saman a few years ago. Didn't they say he was made using the genes of this Eva and Adam Jones?
1000627995.korean: 응. 몇 년 전에 삼안에서 만든 남자 바이오로이드야. 여기 이 에바랑 애덤 존스의 유전자로 만들었댔나?

1000627996.english: - Eva, I’m used to it by now, but this is the first time I’ve heard of a bio-android named Cain. Still, he looks kind of familiar...?
1000627996.korean: - 에바야 이젠 익숙할 정도지만, 케인이라는 바이오로이드에 대해서는 처음 듣는다. 그래도 뭔가 익숙한 모습인데...?

1000627997.english: Huh? I got a message from Sherlock.

1000627999.english: I'm not asking you to buy anything... um...

1000628000.english: I took the phone from Tomo. Only the number '10' is displayed on the screen.
1000628000.korean: - 토모에게서 휴대폰을 받아들었다. 화면에는 '10'이라는 글자만 띄워져있다.

1000628001.english: I don't really make up any kind of secret code with Sherlock, so Tomo doesn't really know either!
1000628001.korean: 셜록이랑은 딱히 암호같은거 안만들어서 토모도 잘 모르겠어!

1000628002.english: Yeah! Ah, the photo is all done.

1000628005.english: Tomo, who was trying to leave in a hurry, bumped into someone, causing the photos to scatter on the floor.
1000628005.korean: - 급하게 나가려던 토모가 누군가와 부딪히는 바람에 사진들이 바닥에 흩어졌다.

1000628007.english: Oh my, why are you so surprised? Did you see a ghost?
1000628007.korean: 어머, 왜 그렇게 놀라시죠? 귀신이라도 보셨나요?

1000628009.english: Thank you for your hard work, Tomo.

1000628011.english: Hehe. It's nice to meet you again, Mr. Watabe Suzuki. I'd love to at least exchange greetings in a relaxed manner, but...
1000628011.korean: 후훗. 또 뵙네요, 와타베 스즈키 씨. 여유롭게 인사라도 나누고 싶지만...

1000628012.english: Watson, you have to run away!

1000628013.english: - Before Shirayuri could even finish speaking, Tomo grabbed me and dragged me along.
1000628013.korean: - 시라유리의 말이 끝나기도 전에 토모가 나를 잡아끌었다.

1000628014.english: While being dragged out through a back door I didn't even know existed, I saw AGS security guards entering the convenience store.
1000628014.korean: - 있는지도 몰랐던 뒷문으로 끌려나가는 동안 편의점 안으로 들어서는 경비 AGS들이 보였다.

1000628015.english: Tomo's expression as he ran holding my hand was more serious than ever before.
1000628015.korean: - 내 손을 잡고 달리는 토모의 표정은 전에없이 진지했다.

1000628016.english: Considering the conversation we had on the way to the convenience store, and the reaction just moments ago... it is clear that there is something I still don't know.
1000628016.korean: - 편의점에 오면서 나눈 대화도 그렇고, 조금 전 반응도 그렇고... 내가 아직 모르는 무언가가 있는 것이 분명하다.

1000628018.english: Sherlock's office was in a complete mess, as if it had been raided.
1000628018.korean: - 셜록의 사무실은 누군가의 습격이라도 받은 듯 난장판이었다.

1000628019.english: While I closed the wide-open window, Tomo headed toward the messy desk with a grim face.
1000628019.korean: - 내가 활짝 열려있는 창문을 닫는 동안 토모는 굳은 얼굴로 어질러진 책상으로 향했다.

1000628020.english: - And, the red dot reflected on the floor-

1000628021.english: At the same time someone embraced me, I was engulfed in an explosion.
1000628021.korean: - 누군가가 나를 감싸안는 것과 동시에, 나는 폭발에 휩싸였다.

1000628023.english: While disoriented by the tinnitus in my ears, I confirmed that my arms and legs were all moving normally.
1000628023.korean: - 귀에서 들리는 이명에 정신 없는 상태로 팔다리가 전부 정상적으로 움직이는 것을 확인했다.

1000628026.english: - Following the race's gaze, I saw Tomo lying in the rubble.
1000628026.korean: - 레이스의 시선을 따라가니 잔해에 깔려있는 토모의 모습이 보였다.

1000628029.english: "I winked and the race disappeared. Together, we cleared the concrete rubble and rescued Tomo, who lay limp.
1000628029.korean: - 내가 눈짓하자 이내 레이스는 모습을 감췄다. 둘이 함께 콘크리트 잔해를 들어낸 후 힘없이 늘어진 토모를 구출했다.

1000628031.english: Yeah... I'm just a little dizzy. I hit my head...
1000628031.korean: 응... 그냥 조금 어지러워. 머리를 부딪혀서...

1000628032.english: - If they're going to plant a bomb so openly, the surrounding area must be surrounded.
1000628032.korean: - 이렇게 대놓고 폭탄을 설치해 둘 정도라면 주변도 포위되어 있겠지.

1000628033.english: - Just as I was about to ask Race to help me find an escape route, I heard the elegant sound of shoes.
1000628033.korean: - 레이스에게 탈출 경로를 찾아달라고 부탁하려는 순간, 우아한 구두소리가 들렸다.

1000628034.english: We didn't know you were an enhanced human... Did you receive the procedure through a route we don't know about?
1000628034.korean: 당신이 강화인간이라는 정보는 없었는데... 저희가 모르는 루트로 시술받으셨나요?

1000628036.english: - Shirayuri shrugged her shoulders at Tomo, who was staggering to get up.
1000628036.korean: - 비틀거리며 일어나는 토모에게 시라유리는 어깨를 으쓱해보였다.

1000628037.english: You know what happens if you turn Kirishima into an enemy, right?
1000628037.korean: 키리시마를 적으로 돌리면 어떻게 되는지, 당신도 잘 알잖아요?

1000628039.english: I can't allow that, Miss Tomo.
1000628039.korean: 그건 허락할 수 없어요, 토모 양.

1000628040.english: As the supervisor of this operation, and as your friend.

1000628042.english: You've accomplished your mission brilliantly. Thanks to Mr. Kimura's information, Densetsu's public image will be tarnished, and our company will be able to successfully enter this country.
1000628042.korean: 당신은 임무를 훌륭하게 완수했어요. 키무라 씨의 정보 덕분에 덴세츠의 대외 이미지는 추락하고, 저희 회사는 이 나라에 성공적으로 진입할 수 있겠죠.

1000628043.english: And Mr. Kimura will be remembered as a righteous journalist who exposed this corruption to the world. In return... he should keep his mouth shut.
1000628043.korean: 그리고 키무라 씨는 세상에 이런 부패를 알린 정의로운 기자로 기억될 거예요. 그 대신... 입을 다물어줘야겠지만요.

1000628045.english: ...I'm an idiot, I don't know about that. I'm going to go save Kimura.
1000628045.korean: ...난 바보니까, 그런 건 몰라. 키무라를 구하러 갈 거야.

1000628047.english: Then would you like to go first? I'll follow shortly.
1000628047.korean: 그럼 먼저 출발하시겠어요? 저는 곧 뒤따라가죠.

1000628048.english: - Saying that, Shirayuri aimed her bow at me.
1000628048.korean: - 그렇게 말하며 시라유리는 내게 활을 겨눴다.

1000628049.english: Originally, eliminating Mr. Suzuki Watabe was the last thing on the plan... but as the supervisor, let's change the order a bit.
1000628049.korean: 원래 와타베 스즈키 씨를 제거하는 것이 계획의 마지막이지만... 감독관 권한으로 순서를 좀 바꾸도록 하죠.

1000628051.english: This person is already too deeply involved.

1000628052.english: Tomo-san, you haven't forgotten our motto while you're acting like a fool, have you?
1000628052.korean: 토모 양. 바보 연기를 하느라 저희의 모토도 잊어버리신 건 아니겠죠?

1000628053.english: - Shirayuri's eyes narrowed at the words I muttered without realizing it.
1000628053.korean: - 나도 모르게 중얼거린 말에 시라유리의 눈이 가늘어졌다.

1000628055.english: Hehe. The more I get to know you, the more interesting you become. What don't you know?
1000628055.korean: 후훗. 알 수록 재밌는 분이시네요. 당신은 대체 모르는 게 뭔가요?

1000628056.english: She's definitely my style. If we had met a little differently, I might have fallen in love at first sight and given myself over to her, body and soul.
1000628056.korean: 정말 제 취향이에요. 조금 다르게 만났다면, 한눈에 반해서 몸도 마음도 다 바쳤을지도요.

1000628058.english: - I felt the sound of Race, who had been hiding his body and blocking my way, raising his gun like lightning, and my vision went blank.
1000628058.korean: - 내 앞을 막아선 채 몸을 숨기고있던 레이스가 번개같이 총을 들어올리는 기척이 느껴짐과 동시에, 시야가 새하얘졌다.

1000628059.english: - And then... someone's memories started to play.

1000628062.english: - The AGS are chasing after Tomo, who is running away, gasping for breath, carrying his briefcase.
1000628062.korean: - 서류가방을 안고 숨을 헐떡이며 도망치는 토모의 뒤를 AGS들이 쫓고 있다.

1000628063.english: - The AGS and nearby factories have the Densetsu mark engraved on them.
1000628063.korean: - AGS들과 근처의 공장엔 덴세츠의 마크가 새겨져있다.

1000628066.english: - Perhaps deciding that he couldn't run away forever, Tomo turned around and raised his submachine gun.
1000628066.korean: - 끝까지 도망칠 수는 없겠다고 판단한 것인지, 돌아선 토모가 기관단총을 들어올렸다.

1000628067.english: - Tomo, who escaped from the factory area, is staggering through the alley at night.
1000628067.korean: - 공장지대에서 빠져나온 토모는 비틀거리며 밤의 골목을 걷고 있다.

1000628068.english: - His entire body was already covered in wounds. Tomo, who had been struggling to move his left leg, finally collapsed to the floor.
1000628068.korean: - 온 몸은 이미 상처 투성이. 결국 움직이지 않게 된 왼쪽 다리를 질질 끌며 안간힘을 쓰던 토모는 결국 바닥에 털썩 주저앉았다.

1000628069.english: Oh my... I'm dying from pain...

1000628070.english: - His body was growing colder from the excessive loss. After a moment of hesitation, Tomo took the documents out of his briefcase, tore them into pieces, and threw them into the sewer.
1000628070.korean: - 너무 많은 출혈로 몸은 점점 차가워졌다. 잠시 고민하던 토모는 서류가방에서 꺼낸 문서를 갈갈이 찢어 하수도에 버렸다.

1000628071.english: Sigh... This is going to hurt more... I really don't like it...
1000628071.korean: 하아... 이건 더 아프겠지... 진짜 싫다...

1000628072.english: - Tomo muttered and put the muzzle of the submachine gun in his mouth.
1000628072.korean: - 토모는 중얼거리며 기관단총의 총구를 입에 물었다.

1000628073.english: - Such a person does not exist. Bioroids of the 080 Institute who fail their mission must erase their own existence.
1000628073.korean: - 그러한 자는 존재하지 않는다. 임무에 실패한 080기관의 바이오로이드는, 반드시 스스로 자신의 존재를 지워야만 한다.

1000628074.english: - I pulled the trigger with my fingers growing weaker and weaker, but instead of the sharp sound of gunshots, only a hollow metallic sound rang out in the alley.
1000628074.korean: - 점점 힘이빠지는 손가락으로 방아쇠를 당겼지만, 골목엔 날카로운 총성 대신 공허한 금속음만이 울렸다.

1000628075.english: I've used up all my bullets...

1000628076.english: Hehehe, then you should die a little slower.
1000628076.korean: 헤헷, 그러면 좀 천천히 죽지 뭐.

1000628077.english: Shirayuri, I'm sorry. The mission... failed...

1000628078.english: - Tomo slowly closed his eyes and after a while, someone came from the end of the alley, the sound of footsteps echoing.
1000628078.korean: - 토모가 천천히 눈을 감고 얼마나 지났을까, 누군가가 골목 끝에서 구둣소리를 울리며 다가왔다.

1000628079.english: Whoaaaaah?! What is this, a corpse?!

1000628080.english: Damn, is this a murder case? Why in front of my office... What, what is it?
1000628080.korean: 젠장, 살인사건인가? 왜 하필 내 사무실 앞에서... 뭐, 뭐야.

1000628084.english: - Tomo woke up to the smell of coffee, the sound of typing, and excruciating pain running through his body.
1000628084.korean: - 토모는 커피 향과 타자 소리, 그리고 온 몸을 달리는 격통에 잠에서 깼다.

1000628086.english: - Realizing that he was lying on the sofa in a very messy room, Tomo instinctively reached for his gun, but only found the old blanket sliding down.
1000628086.korean: - 자신이 몹시 어수선한 방 안의 소파에 누워있다는 것을 인식한 토모는 반사적으로 총을 찾았지만, 낡은 담요가 스르르 흘러내릴 뿐이었다.

1000628088.english: A man sat at a neatly organized desk, typing away at his keyboard. Tomo stood up silently and picked up a ballpoint pen that had fallen to the floor.
1000628088.korean: - 깔끔하게 정리된 책상에 앉은 남자가 키보드를 두드리고 있다. 토모는 소리없이 일어나 바닥에 떨어져있는 볼펜을 주웠다.

1000628090.english: - The man's body trembled as the ballpoint pen tip touched the back of his neck.
1000628090.korean: - 볼펜 심이 뒷목에 닿자 남자의 몸이 움찔 떨렸다.

1000628091.english: What, what is it? Are you awake?

1000628092.english: Who are you? And tell me where this is.
1000628092.korean: 넌 누구야? 그리고 여긴 어딘지 말해.

1000628093.english: Okay, let's take this off first... Ah, okay. Okay, I got it.
1000628093.korean: 이, 일단 이것좀 떼고... 아, 알았어. 알았다고.

1000628094.english: I'm... well, Sherlock Kimura. And this is my office.
1000628094.korean: 나는... 음, 셜록 키무라다. 그리고 여긴 내 사무실이지.

1000628095.english: Okay, Sherlock. Where do you belong?

1000628096.english: Huh? I'm affiliated with the BHK newspaper.

1000628098.english: - Tomo muttered and looked at the monitor screen.

1000628100.english: That's true. If I'd known you were going to shove a ballpoint pen tip down my throat, I would have thought about it more carefully.
1000628100.korean: 그러게나 말이다. 내 목에 볼펜심을 쑤셔박을 거라는 걸 알았으면 좀 더 고민해 봤을텐데.

1000628104.english: I was just curious! I went out to throw out the trash and saw a girl with a gun lying there, covered in blood. How could I ignore that?
1000628104.korean: 궁금해서 그랬다! 쓰레기 버리러 나갔다가 총을 든 여학생이 피투성이가 돼서 쓰러져 있는걸 봤는데 그걸 어떻게 무시하겠어!

1000628106.english: - At that moment, Tomo's stomach started growling.
1000628106.korean: - 그 순간 토모의 뱃속에서 꼬르륵 소리가 울렸다.

1000628108.english: You must be hungry. Do you know how out of your mind you are?
1000628108.korean: 배고플만도 하지. 네가 얼마나 정신을 잃고 있는지 아나?

1000628109.english: If you're going to interrogate me like that, let's do it while we're eating. If I keep having that pen tip pressed down on my throat, I think I'm going to really wet my pants.
1000628109.korean: 그렇게 취조할거면 밥이나 먹으면서 하자고. 계속 목에 볼펜심이 눌린 채로 있다간 진짜로 바지에 실례할 것 같으니까.

1000628113.english: Did you read an outdated novel? Isekai where female students fight with guns are common, though.
1000628113.korean: 철 지난 소설책이라도 봤나? 여학생이 총 들고 싸우는 이세계는 흔하긴 한데.

1000628115.english: Ah, that's a hamburger. I guarantee the taste.
1000628115.korean: 아아, 그건 햄버거라는 거다. 맛은 보장하지.

1000628116.english: Tomo, who had been staring at the hamburger for a while, soon clumsily peeled off the wrapper and took a bite.
1000628116.korean: - 한동안 햄버거를 노려보던 토모는 이내 서툴게 포장지를 벗기고 한 입 베어물었다.

1000628117.english: Tomo, who had been eating a hamburger in silence for a while with a slightly relaxed expression, opened his mouth.
1000628117.korean: - 조금 풀어진 얼굴로 한동안 말없이 햄버거를 먹던 토모가 입을 열었다.

1000628121.english: You've been the only one asking questions for a while now; isn't that unfair?
1000628121.korean: 아까부터 계속 너만 질문하는데, 불공평하지 않나?

1000628122.english: Let's just ask each other one by one.

1000628127.english: What? It's not there? Is it inside the drawer?

1000628128.english: It's my turn. Why was I lying there?
1000628128.korean: 내 차례다. 왜 거기 쓰러져 있었지?

1000628129.english: ...I can't answer that. Where is my gun!

1000628131.english: Haa, well, fine. It's in that drawer.
1000628131.korean: 하아, 뭐 좋아. 그 서랍장에 있다.

1000628133.english: What, what? It's all broken apart!

1000628134.english: I took it apart out of curiosity, but failed to reassemble it.

1000628135.english: It's my turn again. You're a bioroid, aren't you?

1000628137.english: Which company do they work for? Saman? Black River? Or Densetsu?
1000628137.korean: 어디 회사 소속이지? 삼안? 블랙리버? 아니면 덴세츠?

1000628139.english: That means it is a bioroid.

1000628141.english: Sherlock continued speaking without even glancing at Tomo, who was hurriedly covering his mouth.
1000628141.korean: - 셜록은 황급히 입을 막는 토모에게 눈길도 주지 않고 말을 이었다.

1000628142.english: Don't worry about it. Anyone would know just by seeing how it survived intact with injuries like that. And when I carried it, it was surprisingly light for its size.
1000628142.korean: 신경쓰지 마. 그런 상처를 입고도 멀쩡히 살아남는 걸 보면 누구라도 알 수 있다. 그리고 업었을 때 몸집에 비해 너무 가볍더군.

1000628144.english: Don't worry too much. As long as you haven't committed a crime, like running away from the factory or something, I don't really care.
1000628144.korean: 너무 걱정하지 마라. 공장에서 도망쳤든 뭐든 범죄를 저지르고 온 것만 아니라면 딱히 신경 안쓰니까.

1000628146.english: Didn't you say you were a reporter earlier?

1000628147.english: - Saying so, Sherlock turned his chair and turned back to the monitor.
1000628147.korean: - 그렇게 말하며 셜록은 의자를 돌려 다시 모니터로 향했다.

1000628148.english: If you're done eating, you can sleep more, or you can leave whenever you want. I won't catch you.
1000628148.korean: 다 먹었으면 더 자던가, 나가고 싶으면 언제든 나가도 된다. 안 잡을 테니까.

1000628149.english: - Tomo blankly stared at Sherlock for a while, who seemed to be disinterested and was now back to work, before carefully curling up on the sofa and falling asleep.
1000628149.korean: - 관심 없다는 듯 다시 일에 열중하는 셜록을 한동안 멍하니 바라보던 토모는 조심스레 소파에 웅크려 잠들었다.

1000628151.english: - The next morning, the two sat across from each other at the table and ate breakfast in silence for a while.
1000628151.korean: - 다음날 아침, 탁자에 마주 앉은 둘은 한동안 말없이 아침을 먹었다.

1000628152.english: - Tomo's gaze, which had been observing the uncomfortable atmosphere, turned to the monitor that was on.
1000628152.korean: - 불편한 분위기에 눈치를 보던 토모의 시선이 켜져있는 모니터로 향했다.

1000628153.english: Hey, Sherlock Kimura. You're writing about the Densetsu factory fire, right?
1000628153.korean: 저기, 샬록 키무라. 덴세츠 공장 화재 사건에 대한 기사를 쓰는 거지?

1000628156.english: That's not a fire, it's arson.

1000628158.english: Look at the picture. If there was a fire in the factory, the sprinklers would activate and the AGS would put out the fire initially.
1000628158.korean: 사진을 봐. 공장에 화재가 일어났다면 스프링클러가 작동하고 AGS들이 초기진압을 할 거야.

1000628159.english: But the AGSs are not visible in the photo. This is evidence that they were somewhere else.
1000628159.korean: 그런데 사진에는 AGS들이 보이지 않아. 다른 어딘가에 가있었다는 증거지.

1000628160.english: Can we conclude that it was an arson case simply because AGS was not captured in the photo?
1000628160.korean: 단순히 사진에 AGS가 찍히지 않았다고 해서 방화 사건이라고 단정할 수는 없지 않나?

1000628161.english: There's more. Look at the factory ceiling.

1000628162.english: The ceiling has collapsed beyond the area where the fire spread. This doesn't seem to have been caused by a fire, but rather by an explosion or something that tore it apart.
1000628162.korean: 천장이 불이 번진 부분보다 넓게 내려앉아 있잖아? 이건 화재로 천장이 무너진 게 아니라 폭발 따위에 휘말려서 뜯겨나간 것에 가까워.

1000628164.english: That's a credible hypothesis. It'll enrich the article.
1000628164.korean: 신빙성이 있는 가설이군. 기사가 더 풍성해지겠어.

1000628166.english: It was just... paying for the meal.

1000628167.english: I see. Well, I guess the amount is roughly correct.

1000628168.english: Then I'll write the article later...

1000628170.english: It's a fighting game. I bought it to practice because I was upset about losing to a kid at the arcade before.
1000628170.korean: 격투 게임이다. 전에 아케이드 센터에서 꼬마애한테 진 게 분해서 연습하려고 샀지.

1000628171.english: Thanks to that, this month is a fiscal tightening... but I hope it's worth it.
1000628171.korean: 덕분에 이번 달은 재정긴축이다만... 그만한 가치를 했으면 좋겠군.

1000628173.english: Hey, can I do that too?

1000628174.english: It doesn't matter. It'll be better practice than AI.

1000628176.english: Why isn't my ultimate skill working...?

1000628179.english: I told you I lost to a kid.

1000628180.english: I think you're better than that kid.
1000628180.korean: 네가 그 어린애보다 잘하는 것 같지만.

1000628182.english: I know it's fun to win, but let's do it next time. I have to finish writing the article.
1000628182.korean: 이겨서 재밌는 건 알겠다만, 다음에 하지. 기사를 마저 써야하니까.

1000628184.english: Can I stay here a little longer?
1000628184.korean: 나, 여기 좀 더 있어도 돼?

1000628186.english: Sherlock~ Hamburger for lunch today- Whoa?!

1000628187.english: Come in there for a minute.

1000628190.english: Have you seen any injured Bioroids around here in the last week or so?
1000628190.korean: 혹시 지난주 쯤에 이 근처에서 다친 바이오로이드를 보지 못하셨습니까?

1000628193.english: Did the owner lose it around here? It wasn't caught on CCTV, was it?
1000628193.korean: 주인이 이 근처에서 잃어버렸나요? CCTV에 찍히진 않았구요?

1000628194.english: It just so happens that the CCTV in the area was broken that day. Thanks to that, we're able to do all this work...
1000628194.korean: 마침 그날 일대 CCTV가 고장났다고 합니다. 덕분에 저희가 이렇게 발품을 팔고 있습니다만...

1000628195.english: If you happen to see it later, could you please notify the nearest police station?
1000628195.korean: 혹시 나중에라도 보신다면 근처 파출소에 알려주시겠습니까?

1000628196.english: Yes, I will do that. Thank you for your hard work.

1000628198.english: Well, I'm sorry for the inconvenience.

1000628202.english: It's fortunate you weren't caught on CCTV. Seeing as they even mobilized the police, it seems they have no idea where you are.
1000628202.korean: CCTV에 찍히지 않았다니 다행이군. 경찰까지 동원하는 걸 보니 네 행방에 대해 전혀 짐작하지 못하는 모양이다.

1000628204.english: If you keep hiding me... I'm sure later...

1000628205.english: I told you to do whatever you want with it since I didn't care last time.
1000628205.korean: 저번에 신경 안쓰니까 계속 있던 말던 마음대로 하라고 했잖아.

1000628206.english: You help me with work, and it costs money - it costs a lot of money.
1000628206.korean: 네가 일도 도와주고, 뭐 돈이 드는- 돈은 좀 드는군.

1000628207.english: Anyway, even though I'm just living, it's not so bad that I'd sell out my friends.
1000628207.korean: 어쨌든, 내가 막 살긴 해도 친구를 팔아먹을 정도로 막장은 아니라고.

1000628209.english: Right. Hmm, come to think of it, it's similar to your name.
1000628209.korean: 그래. 흠, 그러고 보니 네 이름이랑 비슷하군.

1000628210.english: And the hamburger is off the table today. I have no money because the article I wrote the day before yesterday flopped.
1000628210.korean: 그리고 오늘 햄버거는 기각이다. 그저께 쓴 기사가 망해서 돈이 없어.

1000628211.english: Just then, at a nearby mart, on special... Tomo?

1000628214.english: What, what is this? Why are you acting so scary?

1000628216.english: Did something go wrong with your head when you got hurt last time?
1000628216.korean: 저번에 다쳤을 때 머리에도 문제가 생긴건가?

1000628217.english: Yeah. A guy named Watabe Suzuki is supposed to come help this time, and since he knows a lot of things, let's ask him to take a look.
1000628217.korean: 그래. 이번에 와타베 스즈키라는 녀석이 도와주러 오기로 했는데, 그녀석이 이것저것 아는게 많으니 한 번 봐달라고 하자고.

1000628218.english: He's a good guy, so Tomo, you'll be able to get close to him quickly.
1000628218.korean: 좋은 녀석이니까 토모 너도 금방 친해질 수 있을거다.

1000628220.english: I'll be looking forward to it.

1000628223.english: ...So you were hiding such a trick.

1000628224.english: With a dazed mind, my spirit returned to the present.
1000628224.korean: - 멍한 머리와 함께 정신은 다시 현재로 돌아왔다.

1000628225.english: That's a relief... The commander suddenly staggered and collapsed...

1000628227.english: That video from a moment ago... must be Tomo's past.
1000628227.korean: - 조금 전의 그 영상은... 토모의 과거겠지.

1000628228.english: Was it something that displayed automatically when certain conditions were met? Or was someone...
1000628228.korean: - 어떤 조건에 도달하면 자동으로 보여주는 거였을까? 아니면 누군가가...

1000628229.english: I assessed the situation while being held in Lace's arms.
1000628229.korean: - 레이스의 품에 안긴 채로 상황을 파악했다.

1000628230.english: In fact, not much time had passed, and Shirayuri was looking at Tomo with a troubled expression, holding the bow that had been broken in two.
1000628230.korean: - 실제로 시간은 얼마 흐르지 않았는지, 시라유리는 두 동강 난 활을 들고 착잡한 얼굴로 토모를 바라보고 있었다.

1000628231.english: ...I ended up not being able to kill Mr. Watabe Suzuki.
1000628231.korean: ...저는 와타베 스즈키 씨를 죽일 수 없게 되었네요.

1000628232.english: Miss Tomo. It is an order from the organization. Kill Watabe Suzuki. Then, you just need to return to the designated point.
1000628232.korean: 토모 양. 기관의 명령이에요. 와타베 스즈키를 죽이세요. 그리고 약속된 지점으로 복귀하시면 돼요.

1000628233.english: I don't like it. I won't do it.

1000628235.english: Miss Tomo. The organization tried to eliminate you when you failed your mission to infiltrate the Densetsu factory and disappeared in the past.
1000628235.korean: 토모 양. 예전에 당신이 덴세츠 공장 잠입 임무에 실패하고 사라졌을 때 기관은 당신을 제거하려고 했어요.

1000628236.english: However, you survived because I appealed to you by saying that you would be helpful in successfully executing another plan using Kimura.
1000628236.korean: 하지만 키무라를 이용한 또다른 계획을 성공시키는데에 당신이 도움이 될 것이라는 걸 제가 어필해서 당신은 살아남게 되었구요.

1000628237.english: I didn't explicitly say it, but I'm sure you would have noticed at least that much, right?
1000628237.korean: 굳이 말로 하지는 않았지만, 당신이라면 그정도는 눈치채고 있었겠죠?

1000628239.english: Okay. It looks like you haven't completely lost your mind.
1000628239.korean: 좋아요. 완전히 바보가 된 건 아닌 모양이군요.

1000628241.english: Watson and Kimura are both my friends!

1000628244.english: Tomo had fun spending time with Watson and Kimura.

1000628245.english: I ate warm food for the first time... and played games...
1000628245.korean: 처음으로 따뜻한 음식을 먹어보고... 게임도 하고...

1000628246.english: I made a friend for the first time.

1000628250.english: What do you plan to do without any weapons!
1000628250.korean: 무기도 없이 뭘 어떻게 하겠다는 거죠!

1000628251.english: - Along with Shirayuri's words, AGS units appeared outside the broken office.
1000628251.korean: - 시라유리의 말과 함께 부서진 사무실 밖에서 AGS들이 나타났다.

1000628252.english: Race stood in front of me, but the AGSs only aimed their guns and made no other moves.
1000628252.korean: - 레이스가 내 앞을 막아 섰지만, AGS들은 총구를 겨누기만 할 뿐 별다른 움직임이 없었다.

1000628254.english: Without even looking at the AGSs, Tomo took a gun out of a half-broken drawer.
1000628254.korean: - AGS들을 쳐다보지도 않고, 토모는 반 쯤 부서진 서랍장에서 총을 꺼냈다.

1000628256.english: Tomo is going to go save Sherlock!

1000628258.english: Commander, I've taken care of everything.

1000628259.english: - I tried to keep a straight face, but I could see a sense of pride in Race that I couldn't hide.
1000628259.korean: - 애써 무표정으로 일관하려 하고 있었지만, 레이스에게선 숨길 수 없는 뿌듯함이 묻어나왔다.

1000628260.english: ...I guess there's no choice. I have no cards left.
1000628260.korean: ...어쩔 수 없겠군요. 저에겐 이제 남은 카드가 없어요.

1000628261.english: Go ahead, Miss Tomo. Or... you can just shoot me with that gun and go.
1000628261.korean: 가세요, 토모 양. 아니면... 그 총으로 저를 쏘고 가셔도 좋아요.

1000628262.english: There are still bullets left, right?

1000628264.english: - Tomo, who had been silently looking at Shirayuri, turned his head to me.
1000628264.korean: - 말없이 시라유리를 바라보던 토모는 내게 고개를 돌렸다.

1000628265.english: Watson, I'm going to go rescue Sherlock.
1000628265.korean: 왓슨, 난 셜록을 구하러 갈 거야.

1000628266.english: I have a favor to ask. Will you help me?

1000628267.english: - Tomo gave me a quiet smile, something I had never seen before, at my answer.
1000628267.korean: - 토모는 내 대답에 처음 보는 조용한 미소를 지었다.

1000628268.english: Thank you. Shall we go then?

1000628269.english: - I wonder what the point is now, but the race has disappeared again.
1000628269.korean: - 이제 와서 무슨 의미가 있는지 싶지만 레이스는 어느새 다시 모습을 감추었다.

1000628270.english: - Led by Tomo, I left the office building, which was now nothing but rubble, and glanced back over my shoulder.
1000628270.korean: - 토모에게 이끌려 잔해만 남은 사무실 건물을 나와 어깨 너머로 슬쩍 뒤를 돌아보았다.

1000628271.english: - Shirayuri, who was looking at us silently, had a face that looked somehow refreshing and sad.
1000628271.korean: - 말없이 우리를 보는 시라유리는 어쩐지 시원섭섭해보이는 얼굴이었다.

1000628273.english: Watson, Watson. But Watson's subordinate, can he just hide?
1000628273.korean: 왓슨, 왓슨. 그런데 왓슨의 부하라는 사람, 막 숨을 수 있는 거야?

1000628274.english: - While we were moving, Tomo's question, which had suddenly returned to its usual cheerful self, made me feel a pang of pain.
1000628274.korean: - 한창 이동하던 중, 어느새 다시 평소의 발랄한 모습으로 돌아온 토모의 물음에 뜨끔해졌다.

1000628275.english: Ahaha, that's right! Are you still here? Huh? Huh?
1000628275.korean: 아하핫, 맞구나! 지금도 옆에 있어? 응? 응?

1000628277.english: It's a shame... But I have to introduce you next time, right?

1000628278.english: - Tomo, who had been feeling very sorry, quickly regained his energy and began to walk forward.
1000628278.korean: - 못내 아쉬워하던 토모는 금세 기운을 회복해 척척 앞으로 걸어가기 시작했다.

1000628281.english: Commander. It doesn't have to be this way— Huh?!
1000628281.korean: 사, 사령관. 꼭 이래야 할 필요는- 꺅?!

1000628282.english: Race, right? Race helped us out! Hahaha, nice to meet you!
1000628282.korean: 레이스, 맞지? 레이스가 우리를 도와줬던 거구나! 아하하핫, 반가워!

1000628283.english: Hey, let go of this...! Commander...

1000628284.english: Race, at a loss, asked me for help. "That's what Race sounds like when he's surprised..."
1000628284.korean: - 레이스는 어찌할 바를 모르고 내게 도움을 청했다. 레이스는 놀랐을 때 이런 목소리를 내는구나...

1000628285.english: Yes, we are going to rescue our friend now.
1000628285.korean: 있지. 우린 지금 친구를 구하러 가고 있어.

1000628286.english: Race is our friend too, so he'll help us, right?

1000628287.english: I, I am not your friend...

1000628289.english: Look. Watson is Tomo's friend. Race is Watson's friend, right?
1000628289.korean: 봐봐. 왓슨은 토모의 친구. 레이스는 왓슨의 친구잖아?

1000628290.english: Hehehe, so Race is Tomo's friend too!

1000628291.english: ......! How could I be the Commander's friend-

1000628293.english: - After a while of warm conversation, and Race, who couldn't stand the atmosphere, disappeared, Tomo smiled brightly and walked away.
1000628293.korean: - 한동안 훈훈한 대화가 이어지고, 그 분위기를 못이긴 레이스가 모습을 감춘 뒤, 토모는 빙그레 웃으며 척척 발걸음을 옮겼다.

1000628294.english: - But... do you know the destination?

1000628295.english: Um... I have a few thoughts.

1000628296.english: First of all, Kirishima would obviously have a place to hide people. That alone narrows down the options considerably.
1000628296.korean: 일단 당연히 키리시마가 사람을 숨길만한 곳이겠지. 그것만으로도 선택지는 꽤 좁혀져.

1000628297.english: - Tomo's expression darkened slightly as he said that.
1000628297.korean: - 그렇게 말한 토모의 표정이 조금 어두워졌다.

1000628298.english: If you're planning to kill Sherlock right away, we won't be able to find him no matter how hard we try.
1000628298.korean: 만약 셜록을 바로 죽일 생각이라면 우리가 아무리 몸부림쳐도 찾을 수 없을 거야.

1000628299.english: Still... Kirishima will try to find out how far Sherlock has delved, and probably...
1000628299.korean: 그래도... 키리시마는 셜록이 어디까지 파고들었는지 알아내려 할 거고, 아마...

1000628300.english: I think they're torturing Sherlock somewhere.
1000628300.korean: 어딘가에서 셜록을 고문하고 있을 거라고 생각해.

1000628301.english: - If there's a place where Sherlock might be being held...

1000628302.english: That may be true, but I don't think he's the type of person who would do things at home and leave a trace.
1000628302.korean: 그럴 수도 있지만... 자기 집에서 일을 진행해서 흔적을 남길 만한 성격은 아니라고 생각해.

1000628303.english: There are a lot of eyes watching there. Casasagi will be in control of everything, but the risk will be high.
1000628303.korean: 거긴 보는 눈이 많아. 일단 카사사기가 전부 휘어잡고는 있겠지만, 리스크가 클 거야.

1000628305.english: Such pseudo-religions blind people. Their followers are probably more loyal than the yakuza.
1000628305.korean: 그런 사이비 종교는 사람을 맹목적으로 만들어. 아마 거기 교인들은 야쿠자 아저씨들 보다도 충성심이 높을 거야.

1000628307.english: - Tomo pulled his phone out of his pocket. The number 10 he'd received from Sherlock earlier was displayed on the screen.
1000628307.korean: - 토모는 주머니에서 핸드폰을 꺼냈다. 액정엔 아까 셜록에게 받은 10 이라는 문자가 띄워져 있다.

1000628308.english: The number 10 is written like this.

1000628309.english: - Tomo manipulated his cell phone for a moment and then showed it to me.
1000628309.korean: - 토모가 핸드폰을 잠시 조작하더니 나에게 보여주었다.

1000628310.english: Yeah, yeah. Sherlock must have left us a hint. Let's go.
1000628310.korean: 응응. 분명 셜록이 우리에게 힌트를 남긴 거야. 어서 가자.

1000628311.english: - If not now, there's no time to ask.

1000628312.english: - When I called out, Tomo turned to me silently, as if he knew what I was going to say.
1000628312.korean: - 내가 불러세우자 토모는 무슨 말을 할 지 다 안다는 듯한 얼굴로 말없이 내게 몸을 돌렸다.

1000628313.english: Um... I don't know about Tomo.

1000628315.english: Watson too... isn't he hiding a lot from me?
1000628315.korean: 왓슨도... 나한테 많이 숨기고 있지 않아?

1000628316.english: - Before he could even choose his words to respond to the serious look in his eyes, Tomo smiled bashfully again.
1000628316.korean: - 진지한 눈빛에 대답할 말을 고르기도 전에, 토모는 다시 배시시 웃었다.

1000628317.english: Ahahaha. What a strange face. Is Watson starting to resemble Sherlock?
1000628317.korean: 아하하핫. 이상한 얼굴이다. 왓슨도 셜록 닮아가는 거야?

1000628318.english: - Tomo, who had been turning around and walking ahead without asking any more questions, turned his head slightly.
1000628318.korean: - 더이상 캐묻지 않고 빙글 몸을 돌려 앞서나가던 토모가 살짝 고개를 돌렸다.

1000628319.english: Tell me later, after you save Sherlock.

1000628323.english: Are you coming to your senses, Mr. Reporter?

1000628325.english: …It is an honor to meet you, Representative Kirishima.

1000628326.english: Yes, it would be a great honor.

1000628327.english: If I had known this would happen, I would have just accepted the interview request the last time.
1000628327.korean: 이럴 줄 알았으면 저번에 인터뷰 요청이 왔을때 그냥 받아줄 걸 그랬어.

1000628328.english: Congressman, you don't accept requests unless they come from famous reporters or broadcasters.
1000628328.korean: 의원님은 유명한 기자나 방송국의 요청이 아니면 안 받아주시지 않습니까.

1000628329.english: I knew you wouldn't accept a request from a novice like me.
1000628329.korean: 저같은 나부랭이의 요청은 당연히 받지 않으실 줄 알고 있었습니다.

1000628330.english: Hahaha. Right. I'm glad you have a good grasp of your subject.
1000628330.korean: 으하하핫. 그렇지. 자기 주제는 잘 파악하고 있어서 다행이야.

1000628331.english: Still... I guess it's useful for a trash topic.
1000628331.korean: 그래도... 버러지 주제에 나름 쓸만은 하더군.

1000628332.english: I've been looking through the data you've secured. Honestly, I'm amazed. I admit it. I didn't realize it would be that extensive.
1000628332.korean: 자네가 확보한 자료를 좀 살펴봤네. 솔직히 놀라워. 인정하지. 그정도일줄은 몰랐네.

1000628333.english: And among them... the inferences made through the data were interesting. Well, no one would be interested in such trivial things, though.
1000628333.korean: 그리고 그중에서도... 자료를 통해 추측한 내용이 흥미롭더군. 뭐, 그런 글쪼가리에는 아무도 관심을 갖지 않겠지만.

1000628334.english: It's not a guess, it's an inference, a deduction. And...

1000628335.english: Seeing that I've been dragged here, I think I've at least caught your attention, Congressman.
1000628335.korean: 제가 이렇게 끌려온 걸 보니, 적어도 의원님의 관심은 산 것 아닙니까?

1000628336.english: You're not saying a word. Are you still not fully grasping the situation?
1000628336.korean: 한 마디를 안 지는군. 아직 상황 파악이 제대로 안되나?

1000628337.english: With just one wave of my hand, a hole will be blown in your forehead.
1000628337.korean: 내가 손짓 한 번만 하면 자네 이마에 바람구멍이 뚫린다네.

1000628339.english: If I relax even a little, I feel like I'm going to wet my pants, so could you please put a diaper on me?
1000628339.korean: 조금이라도 긴장을 풀면 바지에 실례할 것 같으니, 기저귀라도 하나 채워주시겠습니까?

1000628340.english: Hahahaha. Yeah, that's what a man should do.

1000628341.english: Mr. Kimura. Would you like to work with me?
1000628341.korean: 키무라 군. 자네, 나랑 같이 일해볼 생각 없나?

1000628343.english: No matter how much I think about it, you are too valuable to be used as building material.
1000628343.korean: 아무리 생각해도 자네는 이대로 건축자재가 되기엔 너무 아까운 인재란 말이지.

1000628344.english: What do you need? Money? With the support of major corporations, you can write articles to your heart's content.
1000628344.korean: 뭐가 필요한가. 돈? 대기업들의 후원을 받으며 마음껏 기사를 쓸 수 있을걸세.

1000628345.english: Or maybe a backer? All the politicians will protect you.
1000628345.korean: 아니면 뒷배? 정계의 의원들이 전부 자네를 지켜줄 거야.

1000628346.english: Of course, depending on what you do, you can get both.
1000628346.korean: 물론 자네 하기에 따라서 둘 다 얻을수도 있네.

1000628348.english: Think about it carefully. You're smart, so you probably noticed.
1000628348.korean: 잘 생각하게. 자네는 똑똑하니 눈치 챘을테지.

1000628349.english: This is too big a deal for a third-rate newspaper reporter like Naburangi to handle.
1000628349.korean: 이 건은 삼류 신문사의 기자 나부랭이가 감당하기엔 판이 너무 커.

1000628350.english: Certainly... I have to admit that. The stakes are too high.
1000628350.korean: 확실히... 그건 인정할 수밖에 없군요. 판이 너무 큽니다.

1000628351.english: So... if it is revealed that lawmakers were lining the pockets of corporations, the repercussions will be huge.
1000628351.korean: 그런만큼... 의원들이 기업의 뒷주머니를 채워주고 있었다는 사실이 드러나면 파급력이 크겠죠.

1000628352.english: Tsk tsk. You're a bit short-sighted in strange places. Well, that's to be expected when you're young.
1000628352.korean: 쯧쯧. 자넨 이상한 곳에서 생각이 짧구만. 뭐, 젊으니 그럴 수 있지.

1000628353.english: It's not about filling your pockets. It's about helping each other out.
1000628353.korean: 뒷주머니를 채워주는 게 아닐세. 상부상조 하는 거지.

1000628354.english: Is it mutual assistance to use Bioroids as objects for entertainment and then have them commit massacres among themselves?
1000628354.korean: 바이오로이드를 물건처럼 접대용으로 쓰고, 자기들끼리 학살극을 벌이게 하는 것도 상부상조입니까?

1000628355.korean: 아니, 아니야. 자네는 단단히 잘못 생각하고 있네.

1000628356.english: Company, if you will, Densetsu is a painter. Painters paint with paint, but is there any painter who is grateful to paint?
1000628356.korean: 회사, 덴세츠는 굳이 말하자면 화가일세. 화가는 물감으로 그림을 그리지만, 물감에 감사하는 화가가 있겠나?

1000628357.english: They're just doing what they have to do, using the right tools.
1000628357.korean: 그들은 그저 적절한 도구를 이용해서, 해야하는 일을 하고 있을 뿐이네.

1000628358.english: And it is clear that Densetsu Science will soon have a global impact.
1000628358.korean: 그리고 분명 덴세츠 사이언스는 곧 전 세계에 영향력을 발휘할걸세.

1000628359.english: Even leaving politics aside, as fellow citizens, shouldn't we naturally help them?
1000628359.korean: 정치를 빼고서도, 같은 자국민으로서 당연히 그들을 도와야하지 않겠나?

1000628362.english: The Bible says that God created humans in his own image. And humans created Bioroids in their own image.
1000628362.korean: 성경에는 하느님이 자신의 모습을 본따 인간을 만들었다고 나온다네. 그리고 인간은 자신들의 모습을 본따 바이오로이드를 만들었지.

1000628363.english: Isn't it very similar? We created Bioroids and became gods. To be exact, corporations.
1000628363.korean: 굉장히 비슷하지 않은가? 우린 바이오로이드를 만들어서 신이 된 거야. 정확히는 기업들이.

1000628364.english: Remain human or become a god... When faced with the choice between the two, foolish people choose the former, while less foolish people choose the latter.
1000628364.korean: 인간으로 남을지, 신이 될지... 양자택일중에 멍청한 사람들은 전자를 고르고, 덜 멍청한 사람들은 후자를 고른다네.

1000628365.english: And people like us... choose to rule over God.
1000628365.korean: 그리고 우리같은 사람들은... 신을 지배하는 쪽을 고르지.

1000628367.english: Well, fine. I'll write the article on your behalf.
1000628367.korean: 뭐, 좋습니다. 의원님의 편에 서서 기사를 쓰겠습니다.

1000628368.english: That's a wise decision. Well then-

1000628369.english: The headline of the first article would be, "God exists, and he is Japanese."
1000628369.korean: 첫 기사의 헤드라인은 '신은 존재하며, 그는 일본인이다.'가 되겠군요.

1000628371.english: Sir, if you're going to waste any more time on this guy...
1000628371.korean: 의원님, 이런 녀석에게 더 시간을 낭비하실 바엔...

1000628372.english: I see. I was just getting tired of it.

1000628373.english: Before I die, I have one question to ask.

1000628374.english: Why did you kill Mr. Yamazaki Tooru and Representative Hatomori?
1000628374.korean: 야마자키 토오루 군과 하토모리 의원은 왜 죽이신 겁니까?

1000628375.english: Heh heh, this guy. Don't say such cruel things like, "Kill him."
1000628375.korean: 허헛, 이 사람이. 죽인다니, 그런 살벌한 말 하지 말게나.

1000628377.english: He threatened me because I was at this church with the Prime Minister's wife. Hahaha, isn't he really funny?
1000628377.korean: 내가 이 교회에 총리대신의 부인과 함께 있던 걸 가지고 날 협박하더군. 으하하핫, 정말 재미있는 녀석이지 않나?

1000628379.english: You probably know without me having to say it.

1000628380.english: Sir, I'll take care of the rest.

1000628382.english: Come over to my house once you've roughly finished up. I've prepared the gift I mentioned before.
1000628382.korean: 대충 마무리되면 우리 집으로 오게나. 전에 말한 선물을 준비해뒀으니까.

1000628384.english: - The church of the Kohei Order. I searched the hall where Mass was in full swing for a long time, but I could not find even a trace, let alone Sherlock.
1000628384.korean: - 코헤이 교단의 교회. 미사가 한창인 홀을 한참동안 뒤지고 다녔지만 셜록은 커녕 흔적조차 발견할 수 없었다.

1000628386.english: Even though he has already looked around several times, Tomo is silently searching every nook and cranny.
1000628386.korean: - 이미 몇번이나 돌아봤음에도 토모는 말없이 구석구석을 찾아보고 있다.

1000628387.english: Commander. I think I found it.

1000628388.english: Now, let me hold your hand for a moment.

1000628389.english: Led by Lace, who was still hidden, I crossed the hall.
1000628389.korean: - 여전히 모습을 숨긴 채인 레이스에게 이끌려 홀을 가로질렀다.

1000628390.english: - Cleverly avoiding people's gazes, Lace let go of my hand only after arriving at a corner of a deserted hall.
1000628390.korean: - 교묘하게 사람들의 시선을 피해가고, 인적이 없는 홀에 구석에 도착해서야 레이스는 내 손을 놓았다.

1000628391.english: In a corner of the wall, something that looked like a scratch but was clearly in the shape of letters caught my eye.
1000628391.korean: - 벽의 한 귀퉁이에, 흠집처럼 보이지만 명백히 문자의 형태를 한 것이 눈에 들어왔다.

1000628393.english: Ahaha, to think I missed something like this. Tomo must have really become an idiot.
1000628393.korean: 아하핫, 이런 걸 놓치다니. 토모는 정말 바보가 됐나봐.

1000628395.english: Yeah. That's how it is. I finally found it.

1000628396.english: As Tomo gave a slight push, the wall slowly opened, revealing the stairs leading down to the basement.
1000628396.korean: - 토모가 슬쩍 밀자 벽이 천천히 열리고 지하로 내려가는 계단이 드러났다.

1000628399.english: I ran into Kirishima who was coming up the stairs.

1000628402.english: Even though Tomo aimed his gun, Kirishima merely clicked his tongue nonchalantly.
1000628402.korean: - 토모가 총을 겨눴음에도 키리시마는 아무렇지도 않게 혀를 쯧쯧 찰 뿐이었다.

1000628403.english: As expected, lowly things cannot hide their true nature.
1000628403.korean: 역시 천한 것들은 본성을 숨기질 못하는군.

1000628404.english: I'll be downstairs, so you handle it. I don't mind if there's a bit of a commotion.
1000628404.korean: 아래에 내려가 있을테니 처리하게나. 소란이 좀 나도 상관없네.

1000628407.english: Someone blocked Tomo as he was trying to chase after Kirishima.

1000628408.english: To think they aimed a gun at a devout believer...

1000628409.english: And that too in the Hall of Sacred Light...!

1000628410.english: An unacceptable act. I will pass judgment on behalf of Lord Azazel.
1000628410.korean: 용납할 수 없는 행위, 아자젤님을 대신해 심판을 내리겠습니다.

1000628411.english: Veronica has particularly high combat power even among members of the same rank. It is impossible to face her with the current strength.
1000628411.korean: - 베로니카는 동급의 대원들 중에서도 특히 전투력이 높다. 지금의 전력으로는 도저히 상대가 불가능.

1000628412.english: - To make matters worse, AGS guards were approaching from behind.
1000628412.korean: - 엎친데 덮친 격으로 뒤에서는 경비 AGS들까지 다가왔다.

1000628413.english: While I was trying to come up with a tactic to somehow get past the stairs, Tomo was leisurely rummaging through his shoulder bag.
1000628413.korean: - 내가 어떻게든 계단을 돌파할 전술을 짜내려는 중에, 토모는 여유롭게 숄더백을 뒤적거리고 있었다.

1000628415.english: This has always been the best solution when things get stuck. Well then...
1000628415.korean: 예로부터 막힐 때는 이게 최고였어. 그럼...

1000628417.english: - Hall was thrown into confusion by the grenade Tomo threw.
1000628417.korean: - 토모가 던진 수류탄에 홀은 혼란에 휩싸였다.

1000628418.english: The AGS guards who survived the explosion were quickly dealt with, but what lay ahead of us was...
1000628418.korean: - 폭발에서 살아남은 경비 AGS들은 서둘러 처리했지만 우리 앞에는...

1000628420.english: Wash away this sin with your lives!
1000628420.korean: 이 죄, 당신들의 목숨으로 씻도록 하세요!

1000628424.english: Jumping into a blocked place on your own two feet.

1000628425.english: Let me atone for you first.

1000628427.english: Are you offering a final prayer? The Light is merciful, so even your prayer—
1000628427.korean: 최후의 기도라도 올리는 건가요? 빛은 자비로우시니, 당신의 기도라도-

1000628428.english: The commander said he believed in me.

1000628434.english: - I went down the stairs and found a basement. Sherlock was tied to a chair, unconscious, and bleeding.
1000628434.korean: - 계단으로 내려가 발견한 지하실. 셜록은 의자에 묶여 정신을 잃고 피를 흘리고 있었다.

1000628437.english: What did you do to Sherlock!

1000628438.english: - Tomo, who had rushed towards Kasasagi, kicked him with all his might.
1000628438.korean: - 카사사기에게 달려든 토모가 힘껏 돌려차기를 날렸다.

1000628439.english: - I thought he would break his back and die instantly, but Kasasagi blocked Tomo's kick with his forearm and was fine, only staggering slightly.
1000628439.korean: - 그대로 허리가 부러져 즉사할 것이라고 생각했지만, 토모의 발차기를 팔뚝으로 막아낸 카사사기는 약간 비틀거리기만 할 뿐 멀쩡했다.

1000628440.english: As expected... you were the culprit.

1000628441.english: I thought he was a cute and confident kid, but he was a Bioroid...

1000628442.english: Things are pretending to be human.
1000628442.korean: 물건 따위가 인간 행세를 하고 앉았군.

1000628443.english: You guys, can you handle it?

1000628444.english: Not only did they commit terrorism against a church in broad daylight, but they also assaulted the CEO of a company.
1000628444.korean: 백주대낮에 교회에 테러를 벌인데다, 기업의 회장을 폭행하다니 말이야.

1000628445.english: Looking at those ominous eyes, I wonder if the charges of assault and battery on a member of the House of Representatives might be added.
1000628445.korean: 흉흉한 눈을 보니 중의원 폭행치상 혐의가 추가될지도 모르겠구만.

1000628446.english: ......You will be judged by the law.

1000628447.english: Judgment by the law? I think I deserve a reward instead.
1000628447.korean: 법의 심판? 나는 오히려 상을 받아야 할 것 같은데.

1000628449.english: Congressman, you came here to scold me for being so foolish that I couldn't shake off my yakuza days and would arrest and torture reporters I didn't like.
1000628449.korean: 의원님께선 야쿠자 시절 버릇 못 버려서 마음에 안 드는 기자를 잡아다 고문하던 어리석은 저를 꾸짖으러 오신거잖습니까.

1000628450.english: - You managed to get the words right in that little while.

1000628451.english: That's not it. You have committed a different sin!
1000628451.korean: 그게 아냐. 당신들이 지은 죄는 따로 있어!

1000628453.english: Hey, Kasasagi. Have you done something bad without my knowledge?
1000628453.korean: 이보게, 카사사기. 자네 혹시 나 모르게 나쁜 짓이라도 했나?

1000628454.english: Oh, how could that be? As you well know, sir, we only do legal work.
1000628454.korean: 아이고, 그럴 리가 있습니까. 의원님도 잘 아시다시피 저희는 합법적인 일만 합니다요.

1000628455.english: - Contrary to their words, Kasasagi and Kirishima are looking at Tomo with eyes that say, "If you want to try, go ahead and do it."
1000628455.korean: - 발뺌하는 말과는 달리, 카사사기와 키리시마는 어디 해볼테면 해보라는 눈빛으로 토모를 보고 있다.

1000628458.english: did forget a few times along the way, but this is a game. No matter how high the degree of freedom is, the fact that I’ve made it this far safely means this must be the situation the creator intended.
1000628458.korean: - 중간중간 잊어버리기도 했지만 이건 게임이다. 아무리 자유도가 높다한들, 여기까지 무사히 왔다는 건 이게 제작자가 의도한 상황일테지.

1000628459.english: - Then, the clear conditions for this game are...

1000628461.english: Well then, let's hear it out. There is still time before the police arrive.
1000628461.korean: 그럼 어디 한 번 들어나보지. 아직 경찰이 오려면 시간은 있으니.

1000628462.english: Ah, don't worry too much about that reporter over there. He said he landed just one blow, right?
1000628462.korean: 아, 거기 기자 양반은 너무 걱정하지 말게. 딱 한 방 먹였다고 했지, 아마?

1000628464.english: Even though this guy's punch is strong, it's not enough to send you to the afterlife in one hit, so he probably isn't dead yet.
1000628464.korean: 이 친구 주먹이 맵긴 해도, 한 방에 저승갈 정도는 아니니까 아직 죽진 않았을걸세.

1000628465.english: Kirishima leaned back in his chair as he said that.
1000628465.korean: - 키리시마는 그렇게 말하며 느긋하게 의자에 기댔다.

1000628467.english: You might not know it, but we Yakuza are a people united by a sense of righteousness. We are merciless to our enemies, but we would never betray our family.
1000628467.korean: 자네는 잘 모르겠지만, 우리 야쿠자는 의협심으로 뭉친 사람들이야. 적에게는 가차 없지만, 가족을 배신하는 일은 없어.

1000628468.english: And you're saying I, the leader of the organization, killed a guy who was like a son to me? Aren't you talking too recklessly just because you're backed into a corner?
1000628468.korean: 하물며 조직을 이끄는 내가 아들같은 녀석을 죽였다고? 구석에 몰렸다고 너무 막 이야기하는 것 아닌가?

1000628469.english: There must have been things in the situation so far that could have identified the culprit. Let's think carefully.
1000628469.korean: - 분명 지금까지의 상황중에 범인을 특정할만한 것들이 있었겠지. 잘 생각해보자.

1000628470.english: - An organization like this must be closed off. They killed Toru Yamazaki because he was trying to leave the organization, knowing the internal situation.
1000628470.korean: - 이런 조직은 분명 폐쇄적일 터. 내부 사정을 알고 있는 야마자키 토오루가 조직을 떠나려해서 살해한거다.

1000628471.english: You really don't know anything about the Yakuza.
1000628471.korean: 자네는 야쿠자에 대해 몰라도 한참 모르는군.

1000628472.english: Even if they quit, I can just cut off a finger and kick them out.
1000628472.korean: 만약 그만둔다고 해도, 그냥 손가락 하나 자르고 내쫓으면 그만이야.

1000628473.english: And silencing him... Even though I assigned Toru middle management last time, I clearly told him he was just a low-level employee. Have you already forgotten?
1000628473.korean: 그리고 입을 막는다라... 저번에 토오루 녀석은 중간관리를 맡긴 했어도 말단이라고 분명히 말했을걸세. 벌써 잊었나?

1000628474.english: - Damn it, is this not right? Let's think about it again.

1000628475.english: Toru Yamazaki said that he was clearly holding a middle management position related to illegal gambling.
1000628475.korean: - 야마자키 토오루는 분명 불법 도박과 관련한 중간 관리직을 맡고 있다고 했다.

1000628476.english: - They could have been involved in the flow of funds, even if indirectly, and it would have been easy to siphon off money along the way.
1000628476.korean: - 간접적으로나마 자금의 흐름에 관여할 수 있었을거고, 중간에 돈을 빼돌리기도 쉬웠겠지.

1000628478.english: If I were Toru Yamazaki and had embezzled the money, I would have tried to run as far away as possible.
1000628478.korean: 내가 야마자키 토오루 군이고, 돈을 빼돌렸다면 최대한 멀리 도망가려고 했을걸세.

1000628479.english: There doesn't seem to be any particular reason to stay in the Red Arena. Don't you think so?
1000628479.korean: 굳이 붉은 아레나에 남아있을 이유가 없어보이네만. 안그런가?

1000628480.english: Assemblyman, you are right. They would have run away, if only out of fear of retaliation.
1000628480.korean: 의원님 말씀이 맞습니다. 보복이 두려워서라도 도망갔을 겁니다.

1000628481.english: - It is difficult to refute. Besides, I haven't seen any evidence so far that Toru Yamazaki has embezzled funds...
1000628481.korean: - 반박하기 어렵다. 게다가 지금까지 야마자키 토오루가 자금을 빼돌렸다는 단서는 보지 못했으니...

1000628482.english: The Bible that Toru Yamazaki possessed. It was a token that he was a follower of the Kohei sect.
1000628482.korean: - 야마자키 토오루가 갖고 있던 성경. 그건 코헤이 교단의 신도라는 증표였다.

1000628483.english: And as revealed by the investigation, the church was also a gathering place for high-ranking officials.
1000628483.korean: - 그리고 조사로 밝혀진 사실로 교회는 고위급 인사들의 모임자리이기도 했다.

1000628484.english: - And crucially, the photo I took.

1000628485.english: - A photo showing the Prime Minister's wife and a Densetsu official... In that case, it would not be strange if a figure related to Kirishima and a Densetsu official were together.
1000628485.korean: - 총리대신의 부인과 덴세츠 관계자가 있던 사진... 그렇다면 키리시마와 관련된 인물과 덴세츠 관계자가 함께 있었더라도 이상하지 않다.

1000628486.english: Toru Yamazaki had a friend with whom he could share stories deep enough to show the Bible.
1000628486.korean: - 거기에 야마자키 토오루에겐 성경을 보여줄 정도의 이야기를 나누는 친구가 있었다.

1000628487.english: It is possible that Toru Yamazaki, unaware that it was dangerous information, unwittingly leaked a story about it, and that it reached Kasasagi's ears.
1000628487.korean: - 그것이 위험한 정보인줄 몰랐던 야마자키 토오루는 자기도 모르게 그에 대한 이야기를 흘리고, 그것이 카사사기의 귀에 들어갔을 가능성이 있다.

1000628488.english: Upon hearing my words, Kirishima shrugged his shoulders as if nothing had happened.
1000628488.korean: - 내 말을 들은 키리시마는 아무렇지도 않게 어깨를 으쓱였다.

1000628489.english: I will admit that I am a follower of the Kohei sect. It is a fact known to everyone who knows.
1000628489.korean: 내가 코헤이 교단의 신도라는 것은 인정하겠네. 알 사람은 다 아는 사실이지.

1000628490.english: By the way, do you know what? The Kohei cult is all the rage in political and business circles these days. They say it wouldn't be strange to meet anyone with even a little bit of a name. Sometimes even I get surprised.
1000628490.korean: 그런데 그거 아나? 요새 정계와 재계에선 코헤이 교단이 유행이야. 이름 좀 있는 사람이라면 누구든 만나도 이상하지 않다네. 가끔 나도 깜짝 놀랄 정도라니까.

1000628491.english: If you're a reporter by name, shouldn't you be a bit sensitive to trends? Especially as a young person.
1000628491.korean: 명색이 기자라면 유행에 좀 민감해야 하지 않겠나? 젊은 사람이 말이야.

1000628492.english: You are right, Assemblyman. That is too much of a leap.

1000628493.english: And listen, Chise, I killed Tooru. Since I smuggled out the data, you all know very well, but wasn't there no one else in and out of there except for bioroids?
1000628493.korean: 그리고 말이야, 내가 토오루 군을 죽였다고 치세. 자료를 빼돌렸으니 자네들도 잘 알겠지만, 거긴 바이오로이드 말고는 출입이 없었지 않은가?

1000628494.english: That is exactly right. There was clearly no access to the data Sherlock stole other than by bioroids. In that case...
1000628494.korean: - 그 말대로다. 셜록이 빼돌린 자료에는 분명 바이오로이드 외엔 출입이 없었다. 그렇다면...

1000628495.english: - The way Kasasagi killed Yamazaki Tooru was...

1000628496.english: Have you never been to the Red Arena before?
1000628496.korean: 자네, 혹시 붉은 아레나 경기장에 한 번도 안 가본건가?

1000628497.english: The Red Arena is a dome-shaped stadium. Unless I had wings, this old man doesn't have the strength to climb the outer wall while avoiding the densely packed CCTVs outside.
1000628497.korean: 붉은 아레나는 돔 모양의 경기장이야. 날개라도 달렸으면 모를까, 외부에 빽빽하게 설치된 CCTV를 피해서 외벽을 기어오를만한 기운은 이 늙은이에겐 없다네.

1000628498.english: Is that so? I saw you the other day and you were full of energy.
1000628498.korean: 그런가? 저번에 보니 아주 기운이 넘치던데.

1000628499.english: Hahaha. Perhaps because it is a gift from the Assemblyman, I feel a surge of energy I didn't even have before.
1000628499.korean: 크하핫. 의원님께서 주신 선물이라서 그런지, 없던 기운도 솟아나지 뭡니까.

1000628500.english: Anyway, if I had climbed the stadium wall, I would definitely have been caught on CCTV.
1000628500.korean: 하여튼, 내가 경기장 벽을 기어올랐다면 분명 CCTV에 모습이 찍혔을걸세.

1000628501.english: And make sure you go to the Red Arena, even now. You won't regret it.
1000628501.korean: 그리고 붉은 아레나는 지금이라도 꼭 가보게나. 후회하지 않을 거야.

1000628502.english: Well, I guess it would be after coming out of prison. Isn't that right?

1000628504.english: It certainly doesn't make sense that they climbed over the wall. In that case...
1000628504.korean: - 확실히 담을 넘었다는 건 말이 되지 않는다. 그렇다면...

1000628505.english: That's not a bad idea. I'll keep it in mind if I ever have to kill someone in the Red Arena.
1000628505.korean: 나쁘지 않은 생각이야. 만약 내가 붉은 아레나에서 누굴 죽일 일이 있다면 참고하겠네.

1000628506.english: But listen. Didn't you say that no one other than bioroids entered the place where Toru was killed?
1000628506.korean: 그런데 말일세. 토오루 군이 살해당한 곳은 바이오로이드 말고는 출입이 없었다고 하지 않았나.

1000628507.english: The young man is already forgetful.

1000628508.english: Tsk tsk. I thought it would be fun, but it's disappointing.

1000628509.english: You guys, if you want to pretend to be a detective, you should read a few mystery novels.
1000628509.korean: 자네들. 탐정 흉내를 내려면 추리 소설이라도 몇 편 읽어보는 게 좋겠어.

1000628510.english: Let me bring in a few books so I don't get bored while I'm in prison.
1000628510.korean: 감옥에 있을때 지루하지 않도록 내가 몇 권 반입하게 해주지.

1000628511.english: Hahaha. You are really thoughtful, sir.

1000628512.english: - Ugh... I was so impatient that I ended up making a ridiculous mistake. Let's calm down and think about it again.
1000628512.korean: - 큭... 마음이 급혀져서 어이없는 실수를 하고 말았다. 침착하게 다시 생각해보자.

1000628513.english: - Thinking back to when I was hanging out with Tomo and at school, I realized that in this day and age, people still can't properly distinguish between Bioroids and humans.
1000628513.korean: - 토모와 함께 돌아다녔을 때와 학교에서의 일을 생각하면, 이 시대에선 아직 바이오로이드와 인간을 제대로 구별하지 못한다.

1000628514.english: - Not only humans, but even the security AGS recognized Tomo, who was disguised as a student, as an unidentified person, not as a Bioroid.
1000628514.korean: - 인간은 물론 경비 AGS조차 학생으로 위장한 토모를 신원미상자로 인식했지, 바이오로이드로 인식하지 않았다.

1000628515.english: - It's a bit ambiguous, but I think Shirayuri and our team members are in a similar case.
1000628515.korean: - 조금 애매하지만, 시라유리와 우리 대원들도 비슷한 케이스겠지.

1000628516.english: You tricked the AGS security guard? How can you say that? Did I use someone to hack into it?
1000628516.korean: 경비 AGS를 속였다고? 어떻게 말인가. 내가 사람을 써서 해킹이라도 했나?

1000628517.english: If that's the case, there must have been traces left behind.

1000628518.english: - One piece is missing. How can I pretend to be a Bioroid...?
1000628518.korean: - 한 조각이 부족하다. 어떻게 해야 바이오로이드인 척을 할 수 있지...?

1000628519.english: I'm getting bored. I'll just end it now and wait for the police.
1000628519.korean: 슬슬 지루해지는군. 이제 적당히 끝내고 경찰이나 기다-

1000628521.english: - Tomo, who had been silent for a while, opened his mouth.
1000628521.korean: - 한참을 조용히 있던 토모가 입을 열었다.

1000628523.english: Grandpa, you're an enhanced human, right? The AGS security guards can't tell the difference between enhanced humans injected with Origin Dust and Bioroids.
1000628523.korean: 할아버지, 강화인간이지? 경비 AGS는 오리진 더스트를 주입받은 강화 인간과 바이오로이드를 구분하지 못해.

1000628524.english: So, all Grandpa had to do was call Yamazaki Tooru to a place with no CCTV, at a time when there were no Bioroids, and just go and kill him.
1000628524.korean: 그러니까 할아버지는 야마자키 토오루를 CCTV가 없는 곳에, 바이오로이드 말고는 없는 시간에 불러내고, 그냥 가서 죽이면 됐던 거야.

1000628525.english: I'm a superhuman? Hahaha. I didn't even realize that Origin Dust had entered my body. There was a reason I was so energetic.
1000628525.korean: 내가 강화인간이라고? 크하하핫. 나도 모르는 새에 몸에 오리진 더스트가 들어왔구만. 기운이 넘치던 이유가 있었어.

1000628529.english: Enhanced humans have enhanced muscle strength thanks to Origin Dust, but their bone strength cannot keep up.
1000628529.korean: 강화 인간들은 오리진 더스트 덕분에 근력이 강화되지만, 뼈의 강도가 그걸 제대로 따라가지 못해.

1000628530.english: This causes a lot of problems... One of the biggest problems is that my nails get twisted when I apply force to my hands.
1000628530.korean: 덕분에 여러가지 문제가 생기고... 그 중에 특히 문제가 되는 게 손에 힘을 줄 때 손톱이 뒤틀리는거야.

1000628532.english: Oh, that's not visible to the naked eye, so you have to go to the hospital and get tested to find out, so there's no need to check it now.
1000628532.korean: 아, 그건 눈으로는 안 보이고 병원 가서 검사해봐야 나오니까 지금 확인할 필요는 없어.

1000628534.english: - Kasasagi, who was hastily checking her fingernails, narrowed her eyes at Tomo's teasing words.
1000628534.korean: - 황급히 자신의 손톱을 확인하던 카사사기는 토모의 놀리는 듯한 말에 눈을 가늘게 떴다.

1000628537.english: There's a precision inspection machine at the stadium entrance, so you won't be able to enter... Oh, right.
1000628537.korean: 경기장 입구에는 정밀한 검사기가 있으니까 못 들어갈테고... 아, 그래.

1000628538.english: The warehouse would have been open at that time because Densetsu was checking the inventory of Bioroids in the warehouse.
1000628538.korean: 덴세츠가 그 시간에 창고에서 바이오로이드 재고 조사를 하고 있어서 창고가 열려 있었을거야.

1000628539.english: I know they didn't check the CCTV because the warehouse was far from the scene of the incident... but if they checked there, wouldn't there be a picture of the old man sneaking in?
1000628539.korean: 창고는 사건 현장이랑 거리가 멀어서 CCTV 확인을 안했던 걸로 아는데... 거길 확인해보면 살금살금 들어오는 할아버지가 찍혀있지 않을까?

1000628540.english: - Tomo, who had said that, turned to me.

1000628542.english: - I felt a slightly different atmosphere from Tomo, who was whispering playfully.
1000628542.korean: - 장난스럽게 속삭이는 토모에게서 어쩐지 지금까지와는 조금 다른 분위기가 느껴졌다.

1000628543.english: Hahaha, now I'm finally getting a decent reasoning.

1000628544.english: I think the aguchi is right. Kasasagi, what do you think?
1000628544.korean: 내 생각엔 일단 아귀는 맞는 것 같네만. 카사사기, 자네 생각은 어떤가?

1000628546.english: But there's another murder case, isn't there? Let's hear about that too.
1000628546.korean: 그런데 살인 사건은 또 하나가 있잖나. 어디, 그것도 한 번 들어보지.

1000628547.english: - Good. Judging by the reaction, it seems like we've overcome a hurdle, even with Tomo's help.
1000628547.korean: - 좋아. 반응을 보니 토모의 도움을 받긴 했어도 한 고비는 넘긴 모양이다.

1000628548.english: Hey, listen. Didn't I tell you last time? Congressman Hatomori and I had some recent friction over business, but we still got along pretty well.
1000628548.korean: 이, 이보게. 저번에 말했지 않은가. 하토모리 의원과는 최근 사업 때문에 마찰이 좀 있었지만, 그래도 잘 지내는 편이었다네.

1000628550.english: From simple business friction to feuds with Kirishima, the motives are abundant without even thinking deeply.
1000628550.korean: - 단순히 사업으로 인한 마찰부터, 키리시마와의 불화까지. 동기야 깊이 생각하지 않아도 차고 넘친다.

1000628551.english: - The important thing is to find out how Kasasagi murdered Representative Hatomori.
1000628551.korean: - 중요한 건 카사사기가 어떻게 하토모리 의원을 살해했는지 밝혀내는 것.

1000628552.english: - Come to think of it, there was something that bothered me about the scene of Representative Hatomori's murder.
1000628552.korean: - 그러고 보니, 하토모리 의원의 살해 현장에서 신경쓰이는 점이 있었다.

1000628553.english: - And unlike the scene at the Red Arena, Representative Hatomori's body was clean with only one wound.
1000628553.korean: - 그리고 붉은 아레나 현장과는 다르게 하토모리 의원의 시체 또한 상처 하나만 있을 뿐 깔끔했다.

1000628554.english: - At first, I thought it was a difference in personal grudges, but I guess you could also think that it was because Yamazaki Tooru tried to resist.
1000628554.korean: - 처음엔 개인적인 원한의 차이라고 생각했지만, 야마자키 토오루가 저항하려 했기 때문이라고도 생각할 수 있겠지.

1000628555.english: - And even the two teacups at the scene. At the very least, the culprit is a big shot, someone worthy of a private meeting with Representative Hatomori, and someone who shouldn't be so wary.
1000628555.korean: - 그리고 현장에 있던 두 개의 찻잔까지. 적어도 범인은 하토모리 의원과 독대를 할 만할 정도의 거물이고, 그리 경계하지 않을 만한 인물이다.

1000628557.english: - Now, there's only one thing left. The contract found on the ceiling, and the masked man Race saw...
1000628557.korean: - 이제 남은 것은 하나. 천장 위에서 발견한 계약서와 레이스가 본 복면을 쓴 남자인데...

1000628558.english: - You said he had a slender body, so it couldn't be Kasasagi himself. Then...
1000628558.korean: - 날렵한 몸이라고 했으니 카사사기 본인은 아닐테지. 그러면...

1000628560.english: - At the bar, Kirishima was talking to someone about a military Bioroid contract.
1000628560.korean: - 술집에서 키리시마는 누군가와 군용 바이오로이드 계약 건에 대해 이야기하고 있었다.

1000628561.english: - Given the context, the contract wasn't finalized at that point. However, what was discovered at the scene was a contract...
1000628561.korean: - 맥락을 봤을 때, 그 시점에서 계약이 성사된 건 아니었다. 하지만 현장에서 발견된 것은 계약서...

1000628563.english: Representative Hatomori had already been in the process of signing the contract, and the man who knew about it took care of Representative Hatomori and stole the contract.
1000628563.korean: 하토모리 의원 측에서 이미 계약을 진행하고 있었고, 그걸 안 아저씨는 하토모리 의원을 처리하고 계약서를 빼돌린거야.

1000628564.english: But to leave something so important out... Hehe, isn't your subordinate an idiot too?
1000628564.korean: 그런데 그렇게 중요한 걸 흘리고 가다니... 헤헷, 아저씨 부하도 바보 아냐?

1000628565.english: These guys, let's take a look...!

1000628566.english: Seriously, Kasasagi. You've finally heard a wonderful deduction.
1000628566.korean: 진정하게, 카사사기. 기껏 멋진 추리를 들었지 않은가.

1000628567.english: - Kirishima calmly got up from his chair and dusted off his pants.
1000628567.korean: - 키리시마는 태연하게 의자에서 일어나 바지를 툭툭 털었다.

1000628568.english: As expected, Bioroids are useless. They fail at something so simple.
1000628568.korean: 역시 바이오로이드는 쓸모가 없어. 그렇게 간단한 일도 실패하다니.

1000628569.english: Hmm, it was a Bioroid after all. The footprints were too shallow.
1000628569.korean: 으음, 역시 바이오로이드였구나. 발자국이 너무 얕았어.

1000628570.english: - I thought it was the footprints of the race, but there was something else besides that...
1000628570.korean: - 그건 레이스의 발자국이라고 생각했지만, 그것 외에도 다른 게 있었던거군...

1000628572.english: It's not manly to try to avoid it after coming this far. Isn't that right, Kasasagi?
1000628572.korean: 이렇게까지 나왔는데 발뺌하는 것도 사나이답지 않지. 안그런가, 카사사기?

1000628574.english: - As if sensing something in response to Kirishima's words, Kasasagi's expression hardened.
1000628574.korean: - 키리시마의 말에 무언가를 직감한듯, 카사사기의 표정이 딱딱하게 굳었다.

1000628575.english: You have a choice. Will you be tried for the murder of a member of the House of Representatives, or will you be tried for the murder of two civilians and the destruction of a Bioroid?
1000628575.korean: 선택하게나. 중의원 살해 건으로 재판을 받을지, 민간인 둘 살해와 바이오로이드 하나 손괴 건으로 재판을 받을지.

1000628577.english: The sentence will be slightly longer than for assaulting and threatening civilians... but if you choose the latter, I promise to do my best to show leniency.
1000628577.korean: 민간인 폭행과 협박에 비하면 형량이 조금 늘기는 하겠네만... 후자를 선택하면 선처를 위해 최선을 다하겠다고 약속하지.

1000628578.english: I trust you will make a wise decision.

1000628579.english: - Kasasagi, who was looking at Kirishima with a leisurely, mean smile, soon slowly walked towards us.
1000628579.korean: - 여유롭게 비열한 미소를 짓는 키리시마를 바라보던 카사사기는 이내 천천히 우리 앞으로 걸어왔다.

1000628580.english: It's a tough world for an old guy like me to adapt to, isn't it?
1000628580.korean: 나 같은 늙은이는 참 적응하기 힘든 세상이야. 안그런가?

1000628581.english: I never dreamed I'd be fighting something like a Bioroid my entire life.
1000628581.korean: 한 평생 바이오로이드 같은 것과 싸워야할 줄은 꿈에도 몰랐어.

1000628582.english: Give up? Ha. That's ridiculous. If I give up, who will feed my family?
1000628582.korean: 포기? 하. 웃기는 소리를 하는군. 내가 포기하면 누가 우리 가족들을 먹여살리겠나?

1000628583.english: Why would I get Origin Dust treatment when I'm old and struggle to keep up with the times?
1000628583.korean: 내가 왜 다 늙어서 오리진 더스트 시술을 받고, 시대에 뒤쳐지지 않으려고 발버둥을 쳤겠나.

1000628585.english: - Saying so, Kasasagi drew his sword.

1000628586.english: The tide of the times is this way, and you tried to go against it.
1000628586.korean: 시대의 흐름은 이쪽에 있고, 자네들은 그걸 거스르려했어.

1000628587.english: According to those people... all you can do is get caught in the rapids and drown.
1000628587.korean: 그런 자들의 말로는... 급류에 휘말려 익사하는 것 뿐이다.

1000628588.english: Don't be so resentful. I feel sorry too- Ugh?!
1000628588.korean: 너무 원망하지 말게나. 나도 미안한 마음은 있- 크악?!

1000628589.english: - Kasasagi, who was about to swing his sword, was suddenly blown away and slammed into the wall.
1000628589.korean: - 칼을 휘두르려던 카사사기는 갑자기 날려가 벽에 쳐박혔다.

1000628591.english: No one... can lay a single finger on the Commander...!
1000628591.korean: 사령관에겐... 누구도... 손가락 하나 대지 못해...!

1000628592.english: Lace, who staggered and blocked my path, had a body covered in wounds.
1000628592.korean: - 비틀거리며 내 앞을 막아선 레이스의 몸엔 상처가 가득했다.
